entries.reasoning: The report cites the provided structural metrics correctly and offers coherent reasoning about concentration, but its predicted targets (WETH, 2269, 182, 1599, 3107) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% protocols by weight loss, and some weight-share claims (e.g., WETH ~23.7%) appear unsupported by the input data.

The report cites specific metrics correctly and provides coherent reasoning about concentration risk, but its target predictions (2786, 2269, 4611, 1599) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk protocols, indicating it failed to identify the actual at-risk entities.

The report cites specific metrics (degree Gini, HHI, PageRank, exposure concentration) and offers a coherent narrative, but its predicted targets (WETH, USDT, 2269, 1599, 182) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss protocols, indicating the actual systemic risk drivers were missed.

The report cites the provided metrics correctly and offers a reasonable interpretation of concentration (high degree Gini), but its target list completely misses the ground-truth top-5% loss set and the systemic risk identification is unsupported by forward-looking analysis.

The report cites specific metrics correctly and provides a coherent rationale about concentration, but the predicted targets (WETH, USDT, 2286, 2269, etc.) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating poor predictive accuracy on the actual task.

The report cites specific metrics correctly and reasonably interprets the hub-and-spoke topology, but its target protocols (WETH, 2786, 2269, 182, 1599, 3107) show essentially no overlap with the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss set, indicating poor predictive accuracy on the actual systemic risk question.

The report cites the provided metrics accurately and offers a coherent rationale about concentration, but its identified target protocols (2786, WETH, 2269, etc.) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating poor predictive grounding.

The report cites actual metrics (Gini, HHI, PageRank) and gives a coherent narrative, but only 1 of 5 targets (118) matches the ground truth set, indicating poor predictive accuracy. There's also a contradictory/garbled claim ('top-10 hold ~52.9B share of a 50.14B network').

The report cites the provided metrics correctly and offers a reasonable interpretation of centrality concentration, but its named targets (2786, 2269, USDT, USDC, etc.) do not match any of the ground-truth high-loss protocols, indicating poor predictive grounding.

The report cites specific metrics correctly and offers a coherent narrative about concentration risk, but its named targets (2786, 2269, USDC, 1599) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating its core risk identification is off.

The report correctly cites the network metrics and reasonably interprets the high degree/PageRank Gini as topological concentration, but its predicted target protocols (2786, 5258, 1599, 2269) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic protocols, indicating poor predictive grounding.

The report correctly cites the provided metrics and offers reasonable interpretation of concentration measures, but its identified targets (WETH, 2786, etc.) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating poor predictive accuracy on the core task.

The report cites specific metrics (M7_degree_gini, PageRank, HHI) and provides a coherent structural concentration narrative, but its targets (WETH, 2269, 2786, 182, 1599, 3107) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating it failed to identify the actual high-risk entities.

The report cites the provided metrics accurately and offers a coherent narrative about hub concentration, but none of its named targets (WETH, 2269, 1599, 182) match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating poor predictive grounding.

The report cites specific metrics and offers a coherent structural concentration narrative, but none of its three targets (2269, 2786, 1599) appear in the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk set, indicating poor predictive accuracy of the actual weight-loss risks.

The report cites the provided metrics and offers reasonable interpretation of the extreme degree Gini concentration, but its target protocols (2786, WETH, 382, 5810, USDE) do not match any of the ground-truth systemic-risk protocols, and it includes fabricated specifics (e.g., '$9.8B+ in top 3', '10,816-protocol network') unsupported by the empty network data.

The report cites the provided metrics correctly and offers a coherent narrative on concentration risk, but its identified target protocols (WETH, 2269, 1599, 182) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating it missed the actual at-risk entities.

The report cites the provided metrics correctly and offers reasonable structural interpretation, but its target protocols (WETH, USDT, etc.) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss protocols, and it relies on static concentration measures rather than forward-looking risk over the horizon.

The report cites specific metrics correctly and provides a reasonable concentration narrative, but its predicted target protocols (1599, USDT, 2269, WETH, 182) miss all 10 of the ground-truth top systemic-risk protocols, indicating poor predictive grounding despite plausible-sounding rationale.

The report cites specific metrics (pagerank_gini, degree_gini, HHI, density) and reasons coherently about concentration risk, but its predicted targets (WETH, 2269, 2786, 1599, 182) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth high-loss protocols, indicating it failed to identify the actual systemic risk set.

The report correctly cites concentration metrics and identifies the hub-and-spoke topology, but its named target protocols (WETH, 2269, 182, 1599, 3107) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating it confuses current weight dominance with forward-looking weight loss.

The report cites specific metrics correctly and offers coherent structural reasoning, but its predicted target protocols (2269, WETH, 1599, USDE, 182) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss protocols, indicating poor predictive accuracy on the core task.
